name: Network Address Translation (NAT)
description: Network address translation lets many private hosts share public addresses: the NAT gateway rewrites the source address and port of outbound packets and records the mapping, and for inbound packets it looks up that mapping to rewrite the destination back to the private host, dropping packets with no matching entry.
mermaid:
graph TD
    N1["Host sends packet"]
    N2["NAT gateway intercepts"]
    N3{"Outbound or inbound?"}
    N4["Rewrite source IP:port"]
    N5["Store mapping in table"]
    N6["Send to public Internet"]
    N7["Lookup mapping (inbound)"]
    N8{"Mapping found?"}
    N9["Drop packet"]
    N10["Rewrite dest to private"]
    N11["Packet delivered"]

    N1 --> N2
    N2 --> N3
    N3 -->|outbound| N4
    N4 --> N5
    N5 --> N6
    N6 --> N11
    N3 -->|inbound| N7
    N7 --> N8
    N8 -->|no| N9
    N8 -->|yes| N10
    N10 --> N11

    style N1 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff
    style N2 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N3 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N4 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N5 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N6 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N7 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N8 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N9 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N10 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N11 fill:#b197fc,color:#fff
